author: POLYCARP

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: Chapter VI.—Polycarp is betrayed by a servant.

ccel.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

source.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

title: Chapter VI.—Polycarp is betrayed by a servant.

source.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

verses.text: And when those who sought for him were at hand, he departed to another dwelling, whither his pursuers immediately came after him.
And when they found him not, they seized upon two youths [that were there], one of whom, being subjected to torture, confessed.
It was thus impossible that he should continue hid, since those that betrayed him were of his own household.
The Irenarch then (whose office is the same as that of the Cleronomus), by name Herod, hastened to bring him into the stadium.
[This all happened] that he might fulfil his special lot, being made a partaker of Christ, and that they who betrayed him might undergo the punishment of Judas himself.
